title: Xantolis shweliensis
intro: Xantolis shweliensis is a species of plant in the family Sapotaceae, commonly known as a type of Sapote tree. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of fever, digestive issues, and respiratory problems. The bioactive constituents of Xantolis shweliensis include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Xantolis shweliensis is often prepared as a decoction or infusions, as well as being used to make tinctures and ointments, to take advantage of its purported health benefits.
taxonomy: Xantolis shweliensis is classified under the family Sapotaceae. This classification is based on its botanical characteristics and similarities with other species within the family. Xantolis shweliensis is a genus of flowering plants, and its classification is further grouped into the order Ericales. The classification helps in understanding the evolutionary relationships and identifying the plant's unique features. By categorizing Xantolis shweliensis under Sapotaceae, scientists can better study its behavior, growth, and distribution.
uses: Xantolis shweliensis is found in the Himalayas and the Eastern Himalayas.  Xantolis shweliensis has been found to have various medicinal uses. Its leaves and stems have been used to treat various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues.  The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory properties. Further research is needed to confirm the full extent of its medicinal uses.
